title: armeria scabra var. labradorica
intro: Armeria scabra var. labradorica, commonly known as the Labrador or dwarf thyme, is a low-growing, perennial flowering plant native to the Arctic tundra of North America and Greenland. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in the indigenous communities of the region, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Armeria scabra var. labradorica include its ability to support respiratory health, improve digestion, and act as a natural antiseptic to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
